senses.definition: the way that someone acts, particularly in the presence of others

examples.x: Despite his age, the child displayed mature behavior.
Her behavior towards her friends shows her loyalty.
I was disappointed by his behavior at the family dinner.
It's rare to see such disciplined behavior in a young child.
We are monitoring the patient's behavior closely for any changes.

senses.definition: all the qualities that shape a person's character and make them different from others

examples.x: Despite her shy personality, she's a fantastic performer on stage.
Even though they're twins, their personalities are quite different.
His outgoing personality makes him popular at parties.
People find her independent personality quite appealing.
People have different personalities, yet we all share the same basic needs and desires.

senses.definition: the set of mental qualities that make a certain person different from others

examples.x: Because of his honest character, people trust him implicitly.
He impresses everyone with his hardworking character.
Her character is quite unique, combining both creativity and logical thinking.
His humble character earns him respect from everyone he meets.
She has a very friendly character and easily makes friends.

senses.definition: nervous and uncomfortable around other people

examples.x: Being shy hides his brilliant ideas.
Despite her shy nature, she stepped up to lead the team.
Even though she's shy, she can express herself well through her artwork.
He is shy, but he never hesitates to help others in need.
His shy personality does not stop him from performing on stage.

senses.definition: talking a great deal

examples.x: Even though he's talkative, he knows when to stay quiet.
His talkative nature makes him a great salesperson.
My brother is so talkative that he can keep a conversation going for hours.
She's talkative, which is a great trait for her job as a tour guide.
She's the most talkative person in our group; she always keeps us entertained.

senses.definition: (of a person) quiet, thoughtful, and showing little emotion in one's manner or appearance

examples.x: She is a serious person who focuses on her work without distractions.
He is a serious person who always thinks carefully before making decisions.
She maintained a serious expression throughout the meeting.
His serious nature makes him a great leader in difficult situations.
The serious man listened intently and didn't interrupt during the discussion.
He looked serious during the discussion about the project.
Unlike her friends, she is very serious and rarely smiles during casual conversations.
He became serious when the conversation turned to business.
They seem serious, let's ask if something is wrong.

senses.definition: able to make people laugh

examples.x: He's a funny character, always coming up with quirky ideas.
I find it funny when cats chase their tails.
I read a funny comic strip in the newspaper this morning.
She finds it funny that her dog is afraid of cats.
The cartoon was so funny that I couldn't stop laughing.

senses.definition: catching and keeping our attention because of being unusual, exciting, etc.

examples.x: I read an interesting article about space exploration in the newspaper.
It's interesting to note how technology has evolved over the years.
My neighbor has an interesting collection of vintage cars.
The museum had many interesting exhibits.
The teacher made the lesson interesting by including interactive activities.

senses.definition: making us feel tired and unsatisfied because of not being interesting

examples.x: She finds doing the laundry a boring task.
The class was boring because the teacher simply read from the textbook.
The lecture was so boring that several students left early.
The movie was boring and I fell asleep halfway through it.
The TV show was boring, so I switched the channel.

senses.definition: making us feel interested, happy, and energetic

examples.x: It was exciting to see dolphins while we were on the boat.
The concert was exciting, with amazing performances by my favorite bands.
The fireworks display was really exciting and lit up the whole sky.
The soccer game was very exciting, with both teams scoring in the final minutes.
They're going on an exciting road trip across the country next summer.

senses.definition: very great and pleasant

examples.x: It's a wonderful day outside, with sunny skies and a gentle breeze.
She has a wonderful sense of humor that always makes me laugh.
She has done a wonderful job organizing the event.
We had a wonderful time at the beach this weekend.
We visited some wonderful museums during our trip to London.

senses.definition: having an exceptionally high quality

examples.x: He's an amazing artist who can draw incredibly lifelike portraits.
Her performance in the play was truly amazing.
It's amazing how much information you can find on the internet.
The book was an amazing adventure from beginning to end.
The sunset painted an amazing array of colors across the sky.

senses.definition: very good in quality or other traits

examples.x: He made an excellent point about the importance of recycling.
She is an excellent pianist who has won many competitions.
The festival was excellent, with great music and food.
The hotel provided excellent service during our stay.
The students received excellent grades on their exams.

senses.definition: extremely good and amazing

examples.x: He did an awesome presentation for his final project.
She got an awesome new skateboard for her birthday.
The concert was awesome, with incredible light and sound effects.
The roller coaster ride was awesome and full of twists and turns.
The summer camp was awesome, with so many fun activities to do.

senses.definition: nice and caring toward other people's feelings

examples.x: It's a kind gesture to write thank you notes after receiving gifts.
She has a kind heart and always thinks of others.
She's always kind to everyone she meets.
The teacher was kind enough to give us an extension on the project.

senses.definition: strange in a way that is difficult to understand

examples.x: He's a good friend, but he has some weird tastes in music.
She has a weird habit of talking to herself when she's nervous.
She's a little weird, always wearing mismatched socks on purpose.
The magician did a weird trick that I still can't figure out.
The movie had a weird ending that left the audience confused.

senses.definition: (of a person) without physical or mental problems

examples.x: A normal day for her involves school, homework, and a little bit of TV.
He's a normal kid who loves to play football and video games.
My friends are pretty normal, we enjoy hanging out and talking about our interests.
My neighbor is quite normal, always up early for a jog before work.

senses.definition: having unusual, unexpected, or confusing qualities

examples.x: He has a strange habit of talking to himself when he's working.
I saw a strange bird in the park that I've never seen before.
It's strange that he didn't call, he's usually so punctual.
It's strange to see the streets so empty during a busy time of day.
The soup had a strange color, but it tasted delicious.

senses.definition: providing pleasure and enjoyment

examples.x: The restaurant served a nice meal with fresh ingredients.
She bought a nice jacket made of high-quality leather.
They moved into a nice house with modern appliances.
The store offers a selection of nice products that last longer.
He drives a nice car that always turns heads on the road.

senses.definition: worthy of being approved or admired

examples.x: He's a great boss, always listening to his employees' ideas.
My parents are great, they always support me in everything I do.
She's a great friend, always there when you need her.
That's a great idea for a startup, I think it will be very successful.
This restaurant is great, the food and service are excellent.

senses.definition: (of a person) strong and able to deal with problems

examples.x: Being a firefighter has made him a tough man.
He's a tough negotiator, always getting the best deals.
My friend is tough, nothing ever seems to get her down.
My grandmother is tough, she raised six children on her own.

senses.definition: unlike anything else and distinguished by individuality

examples.x: Each snowflake is unique with its own pattern.
He has a unique habit of sketching people he meets for the first time.
Her unique way of always seeing the positive in every situation makes her stand out.
This artist has a unique perspective that makes her artwork stand out.
This dish has a unique flavor combination that is surprisingly good.

senses.definition: feeling angry and unhappy because someone else has what we want

examples.x: Don't be jealous of his success, you can achieve great things too.
He felt jealous when he saw his best friend talking to his crush.
I'm so jealous of your vacation plans.
She was jealous of her sister's success.
When his coworker got a raise, he couldn't help but feel jealous.

senses.definition: extremely clever, talented, or impressive

examples.x: He's a brilliant coach who always gets the best out of his team.
Everyone admired her for being a brilliant thinker and innovator.
She’s a brilliant leader who inspires her team to achieve great results.
Brilliant as always, she aced the exam with ease.
He’s a brilliant mathematician who solves problems others find impossible.

senses.definition: making use of imagination or innovation in bringing something into existence

examples.x: I believe you are a creative photographer; you always find beauty in ordinary things.
I find my sister a creative person, always coming up with new ideas for products to sell in her shop.
I met a creative artist, turning everyday objects into beautiful sculptures.
My cousin is very creative, she paints beautiful pictures with watercolors.
My friend is very creative, she designed and sewed her own dress for the party.

senses.definition: (of a person) not possessing a stable and healthy mental condition

examples.x: He's acting crazy, he insists he can communicate with his dead cat.
He's not dangerous, just a bit crazy and talks nonsense sometimes.
She's been acting crazy lately, telling us she’s a time traveler from the future.
The lack of sleep was making him feel crazy, he kept seeing shadows moving.
People thought he was crazy for talking to himself all the time.

senses.definition: completely without mistakes or flaws, reaching the best possible standard

examples.x: He's the perfect candidate for the job with all his experience.
Jimmy is the perfect student, always paying attention in class.
My dog is perfect, even though he occasionally chews my shoes.
My grandmother is a perfect host, always making her guests feel welcome.
She's the perfect fit for the team with her positive attitude.

senses.definition: (of a person) having a willingness or readiness to help someone

examples.x: He's been very helpful during the move, carrying the heavy boxes.
My classmate is very helpful, sharing his notes when I miss a lecture.
Please be helpful and clean up after meals.
The librarian was very helpful, helping me find the books I needed.
The shop assistant was very helpful; she found the perfect gift for my mom.

senses.definition: treating everyone equally and in a right or acceptable way

examples.x: All students are treated equally by her because she is a fair teacher.
The interviewer asks each candidate the same questions, proving he is a fair person.
The policy was designed to be fair to all employees, regardless of their position.
They reached a fair agreement that benefited both sides equally.
The judge made a fair ruling, ensuring justice for all involved.

senses.definition: (of a person) having no respect for other people

examples.x: Anna is so rude, she always interrupts when others are speaking.
He's quite rude, always shouting at people for no reason.
He's so rude, he didn't even say hello when he walked in.
She was being rude by constantly texting during the movie.
She's rude and never says please or thank you.

senses.definition: experiencing a lack of joy or positive emotions

examples.x: He felt unhappy in his job despite the high salary.
She seemed unhappy after the phone call.
The dog looked unhappy being left alone all day.
He grew increasingly unhappy with his living situation.

senses.definition: having a strong belief in one's abilities or qualities

examples.x: He's confident about his decision to start a new business.
I'm confident that we can finish the project on time.
She's a confident speaker, never nervous in front of a crowd.
The pilot was confident about landing the plane in the bad weather.
The teacher was confident about her students' progress.

senses.definition: making us feel fear

examples.x: He thinks flying is scary because he's afraid of heights.
I had a scary nightmare about zombies last night.
She thinks thunderstorms are scary.
The dentist is scary for many kids.
The scary dog barked at us as we walked past the house.

senses.definition: (of a person) doing many things with a lot of energy

examples.x: Despite being retired, he remains active, taking part in various community activities.
My grandmother is quite active for her age, she even does gardening.
She's active in the art community, regularly attending exhibitions and workshops.
She's an active traveler, always planning her next adventure.
The active kids played outside all afternoon without getting tired.

senses.definition: having a gentle or not very strong effect

examples.x: Her mild response to criticism shows her maturity.
The medication has a mild effect and doesn't cause drowsiness.
She experienced mild discomfort after the procedure.
His mild reaction to the criticism was unexpected.
The earthquake was mild, causing no significant damage.

senses.definition: given to or related to one single person or thing

examples.x: Each student must submit an individual project.
Our individual actions contribute to the overall success of the team.
She has her individual style of painting.
The trainer provides individual feedback to all the participants.
They put their individual differences aside to work as a team.

senses.definition: displaying poor judgment or a lack of caution

examples.x: It was a foolish gamble to invest all his money in one stock.
She made a foolish promise without considering the consequences.
His foolish attempt to fix the car without experience resulted in damage.
A foolish comment during the interview cost him the job.
The foolish choice to walk alone at night put him in danger.

senses.definition: feeling completely sure about something and showing that you believe it

examples.x: Being certain of her feelings, she decided to confess her love.
He felt certain that the plan would work.
I'm certain we're on the right road.
If you are certain of his loyalty, you can tell him your secret.
She was certain that she left her keys on the table.

senses.definition: (of a person) not talking too much
used for showing that someone or something belongs to or is connected with a particular person or thing

examples.x: Each plant has its own pot in the garden.
She drives her own car to work every day.
The company uses its own software for managing tasks.
The little girl loves to read her own books.
They have their own way of doing things.
